canonical_summary: The ideas-lab project tracks Ameen's work as a Business Analytics Intern at IDeaS Revenue Solutions (IDeaS.Lab). A core deliverable is the Discovery Gap Sentinel — a Teams declarative agent that triggers an analysis pipeline via GitHub Actions and reads report outputs from SharePoint. The sideload package is at v1.0.29 (manifest bumped to fix YAML scope mismatch). The Teams GitHub OAuth integration is BLOCKED: the OAuthPluginVault registration is correctly configured (reference_id matches, app ID d670c019 correct, scope: repo, endpoints correct) but the Teams sign-in card has never appeared — root cause is either M365 tenant policy blocking external OAuth or registration propagation delay (created 5/22/26). No GitHub Actions run has ever been created by the Teams agent; all prior runs were manually triggered. A pivot to ApiKeyPluginVault with a new GitHub PAT is under consideration. Correct 360Chicago session 5 file paths confirmed: data/transcripts/360chicago/. Earlier May 28 sessions: provisioned via M365 Agents Toolkit 6.10.0 (57/57 validations), completed full Python pipeline audit (governance.py abbreviation fix, evidence.py retry fix, check_run_scope.py snapshot/baseline, dead code removal). Test suite: 359 passing. Pipeline health: 9/10.

known_solutions.problem: Teams OAuthPluginVault sign-in card never appears — agent gets 'encountered an error when processing' on all GitHub API calls
known_solutions.solution: Root cause unresolved. OAuthPluginVault registration is correctly configured. Two candidate causes: (1) M365 tenant policy blocking external OAuth sign-in flows in Copilot plugins — needs M365 admin check; (2) registration propagation delay (new registrations can take up to 60 min). Workaround path: switch to ApiKeyPluginVault with a GitHub classic PAT (repo scope) — bypasses sign-in card entirely.

known_solutions.problem: Teams agent dispatch inputs used bare filenames instead of repo-relative paths, causing workflow prepare_analysis step to fail with 'file not found'
known_solutions.solution: 360Chicago brief path: data/transcripts/360chicago/360Chicago_Client_Brief_Strict_Post_Research_Desk_Only_v0x 1.docx; transcript path: data/transcripts/360chicago/session_05_2026-01-15_segmentation-channels.docx. General rule: always use full repo-relative paths from repo root.

known_solutions.problem: governance.py sentence counter fires on abbreviation periods (Dr., Mr., Ms., etc.) causing false 'too many sentences' errors on valid narratives
known_solutions.solution: Add _ABBREVS pattern and _count_sentences() helper that replaces abbreviation periods with null-byte placeholder before splitting on sentence boundaries. Lookbehind regex does NOT work because abbreviations end in lowercase.

known_solutions.problem: check_run_scope.py flags pre-existing uncommitted changes as session violations when working tree is dirty
known_solutions.solution: Use --snapshot FILE before session starts to record pre-existing dirty paths; use --baseline FILE at session end to compare only new changes. Legacy no-arg mode preserved.

known_solutions.problem: evidence.py retry fallback prompt never included item_verdicts, so retry always fails with same 'item_verdicts missing keys' error
known_solutions.solution: Conditionally add item_verdicts schema description to the strict retry prompt when required_items is non-empty, listing exact required keys.

known_solutions.problem: m365agents.yml rejected by M365 Agents Toolkit with InvalidYamlSchemaError
known_solutions.solution: File must use version: v1.11 lifecycle schema format (provision/deploy/publish stages), not custom fields. Toolkit 6.10.0 requires exactly 'v1.11'.

known_solutions.problem: teamsApp/zipAppPackage fails with doubled path 'actions/actions/xxx.yaml not found'
known_solutions.solution: Plugin JSON spec URLs were relative to the plugin file's directory. Since plugin JSONs were in actions/, 'actions/xxx.yaml' doubled. Fix: move plugin JSONs to appPackage root and use 'actions/xxx.yaml' as spec URL.

known_solutions.problem: teamsApp/validateAppPackage fails with 'File not found in zip archive' even though YAML is in the zip
known_solutions.solution: Toolkit zip builder resolves spec URLs relative to plugin JSON directory on disk; validator resolves relative to zip root. Only consistent when plugin JSONs are at appPackage root. Keep plugin JSONs at root, YAML specs in actions/.

known_solutions.problem: Teams Dev Portal OAuth connection rejects requests after provisioning new app
known_solutions.solution: Update the 'Restrict usage by app' field in the OAuth connection to the new provisioned app ID (f6d6a2eb). The old sideload app ID (d670c019) no longer applies.

known_solutions.problem: Need to determine which week folder each IDeaS_Lab file belongs to based on modification date
known_solutions.solution: IDeaS_Workspace contains Week-01_Feb-03 through at least Week-08_Mar-24 folders

known_solutions.problem: Conversation transcript is truncated — only the scheduled task invocation header and an empty synthetic response are visible
known_solutions.solution: No results determinable from the truncated transcript
IDeaS_Workspace not present at expected Desktop path

known_solutions.problem: 360chi health check report files had been pushed to a GitHub repo and the user did not want them traceable
known_solutions.solution: Report files confirmed still present on disk after git rm --cached

known_solutions.problem: The data-qualification-agent repo had been pushed to GitHub with history that the user did not want exposed
known_solutions.solution: Report files (360chi_health_check_report.docx/html/json/md) confirmed still on disk after git rm --cached

decision_log.decision: Remove 'workflow' scope from githubActions.yaml — repo scope is sufficient for workflow_dispatch, listing runs, and downloading artifacts from private repos
OAuthPluginVault approach may need to be abandoned in favor of ApiKeyPluginVault with a static GitHub PAT — Teams OAuth sign-in card consistently fails to appear; pending final call
Sentence counter false-fires fixed via abbreviation pre-processing (_count_sentences helper), not lookbehind regex — lookbehind fails on lowercase-ending abbreviations like Ms., Dr.
validate_report expected_sections defaults to 16 for backward compat; render_from_results.py passes len(registry) to make it data-driven
evidence.py stubbed as 7-line namespace stub (not deleted) to avoid breaking smoke import test
render_html v1 (~985 lines) fully removed from report_builder.py — not kept as named fallback
Plugin JSONs live at appPackage root; YAML specs stay in actions/ — this is the required structure for M365 Agents Toolkit
m365agents.yml must use version: v1.11 and include deploy: [] even for declarative agents with nothing to deploy
GitHub OAuth repo scope is sufficient for workflow_dispatch — workflow scope is for editing workflow YAML files, not running them
Replace existing repo with a clean single-commit repo with no prior history
Keep the repo private going forward
Replace existing repo with a fresh repo that has no prior git history
Keep the repo private going forward

open_threads.question: Should Teams GitHub auth switch from OAuthPluginVault to ApiKeyPluginVault with a GitHub PAT? If so, create a classic PAT with repo scope and register in Teams Dev Portal as API key.
Is M365 tenant policy blocking external OAuth sign-in flows for Copilot plugins? Needs M365 admin check.
Should dispatch go through GitHub API directly or through the existing Lambda/API Gateway endpoint (API_GATEWAY_KEY)?
Does the Agent_B_Specs.docx word document reflect the current Gate 5 pipeline? (user opened it but review was not completed)
Has end-to-end workflow dispatch been tested from the Teams agent through to pipeline completion?
Are the changes on branch fix/apppackage-v0.2.0-sync committed and pushed?
Did the 'ideas-workspace-weekly-sync' scheduled task execute its intended logic, or did it fail silently?
Why does the assistant response show zero input and output tokens?
Were the IDeaS_Lab and Downloads files successfully moved into their correct weekly folders?
Was Week-12_Apr-21 (or the current week folder) created?
Did the scheduled task complete its full sync run or exit partway through?
What is the full content of the ideas-workspace-weekly-sync scheduled task SKILL.md?
Where is the IDeaS_Workspace directory located if not on the Desktop?
Were sensitive files like .claude/settings.local.json and any xlsx files properly excluded from the fresh commit?
Was the fresh repo successfully pushed to GitHub after the history strip?
